title: Incorrect Binary Exponentiation with Repeated Squaring

statement.markdown:
Given the function `solve(n, m)` that calculates `m` power `n` modulo some prime number `p`, the function incorrectly handles the repeated squaring step. Fix the bug to make it work correctly.

The function should return the result of `m` power `n` modulo `p`, where `p` is always an odd prime number.
statement.inputFormat: The input format is `n m p`. The first number is the base `m`, the second number is the power `n`, and the third number is the prime number `p`.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an integer representing the result of `m` power `n` modulo `p`.
statement.notes:
The time limit is 2 seconds and the space limit is 256 MB.
The function should use the repeated squaring method to calculate the result.

hints.text: Use the repeated squaring method to calculate the result.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The function should use the repeated squaring method to calculate the result correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute-force approach would involve iterating over the power `n` times, which is inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the repeated squaring method to calculate the result in O(log n) time.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the repeated squaring method with the correct handling of the power `n`.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Proof of the correctness of the repeated squaring method.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the repeated squaring method.

functionSignature.cpp: long long solve(long long n, long long m, long long p);
functionSignature.java: long solve(long n, long m, long p);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, n: long, m: long, p: long) -> long;
